title: Gas Station
problem_statement: Find the starting gas station index from which you can complete the circuit around all stations once, or return -1 if impossible.

examples.explanation: Starting at station 3 allows completing the circuit and returning to the start.

intuition: Track net fuel and restart from the next station when the current route becomes impossible; the total net fuel determines feasibility.

approaches.name: One-pass station scan
approaches.description: Maintain total and current fuel balance while scanning stations; when the current balance drops below zero, reset the start point and continue.
approaches.why_not: Checking all start points is O(n^2) and unnecessary because a unique valid start exists if one exists.

interview_qa.q: Why can you reset the start after a failed route?
interview_qa.a: If the current route fails at i, none of the stations before i can be a valid start because they would also run out of fuel by i.
The overall feasibility of completing the circuit; if total is negative, no solution exists.
